articles.title: Open Thread
articles.content:
沃洛赫阴谋论
多为法学教授 | 偶尔唱反调 | 常持自由意志主义立场 | 始终保持独立

太多媒体试图告诉你该怎么想。支持帮助你独立思考的新闻报道。我现在就向Reason捐款！不了，谢谢

反击误导性媒体谎言和不良思想。今天就支持Reason的新闻报道。我的捐款将帮助Reason反击！今天不行

支持致力于透明、独立和知识诚信的新闻报道。好的，我今天就向Reason捐款！不了，谢谢

支持挑战中央计划、政府越权和蔓延的社会主义的新闻报道。好的，我今天就支持Reason！不了，谢谢

支持揭露不良经济、失败政策和开放市场威胁的新闻报道。好的，我今天就向Reason捐款！不了，谢谢

支持审视社会主义政策实际后果的独立媒体。好的，我今天就向Reason捐款！不了，谢谢

支持以理性分析和清晰推理挑战政府越权的新闻报道。好的，我今天就向Reason捐款！不了，谢谢

支持挑战中央权力、维护个人自由的新闻报道。好的，我今天就向Reason捐款！不了，谢谢

您的支持有助于揭露社会主义政策提案的真实代价，并提出更好的替代方案。好的，我今天就向Reason捐款！不了，谢谢

articles.title: Federalist Society Courthouse Steps Podcast on Pung v. Isabella County Takings Case
articles.content:
我最近参加了一个联邦党人协会播客节目，讨论Pung v. Isabella County案，这是最高法院最近一项存在严重缺陷的征用条款裁决。太平洋法律基金会的Deborah La Fetra（此案中代表业主的律师之一）也参与了讨论。Deborah对案件发回重审后的最终结果比我更为乐观（尽管我同意她的观点，业主很可能最终会胜诉，因为他们在程序上受到了不公平对待）。不过，我和她都认为，最高法院的这一裁决在涉及可能税务违约情况下政府没收财产时"公正补偿"的计算标准方面，树立了一个糟糕的先例。我之前在这里和这里都写过关于Pung案的文章。

我在下面嵌入了播客视频：

注：太平洋法律基金会也是我妻子的雇主。不过，她不是此案的代理律师。Pung遗产也有其他律师代理。

这篇文章首先出现在Reason.com上，标题为《联邦党人协会 Courthouse Steps 播客：Pung v. Isabella County 征用案》。
articles.summary:
I recently participated in a Federalist Society podcast on Pung v. Isabella County, the Supreme Court's recent badly flawed Takings Clause decision. Deborah La Fetra of the Pacific Legal Foundation (one of the lawyers representing the property owners in the case) also took part. Deborah is a bit more optimistic than me about the ultimate resolution of the case on remand (though I agree with her that the property owners might well ultimately prevail based on the unfairness of the procedures they were subjected to). Regardless, I think we both agree that the Court's decision sets a terrible precedent on the calculation of "just compensation" under the Takings Clause, in cases where the government seizes property due to possible tax delinquencies. I previously wrote about Pung here and here.

I embedded the video of the podcast below:

NOTE: The  Pacific Legal Foundation is also my wife's employer. She, however, is not one of the attorneys on the case. The Pung estate is also represented by other attorneys.

The post Federalist Society Courthouse Steps Podcast on Pung v. Isabella County Takings Case appeared first on Reason.com.

articles.title: A Biohacker Gives Birth
articles.content:
我的六块腹肌在怀孕第四个月开始消失，到第十五周时彻底消失。

怀孕期间，我的腹肌消失只是我作为生物黑客在职业上面临的众多令人沮丧的权衡之一。从青春期起，我就天生拥有可见的腹肌。虽然我想把这归功于我的努力锻炼，但六块腹肌主要只是体重分布和低体脂的产物。你无法控制身体在哪里堆积脂肪，即便是皮下脂肪。但自疫情封控期间锁定生物黑客技术以来，优化身体机能一直是我乐此不疲的爱好。

附注：我不会使用“我们怀孕了”这个说法，因为尼克·吉尔斯皮——他是《理性》杂志的高级编辑，也是我的丈夫——在整个怀孕过程中承受的痛苦微乎其微。

怀孕意味着我对生物黑客的投入会变得更加密集——即便可用的工具会变得越来越不实用。我成了别人的感官剥夺舱。生物黑客变成了被黑客的对象。生物黑客是简单应用科学和技术来按自己意愿改造身体。一个完整的市场已经兴起，让一群新的自我实验者在各种聚会上变得极其令人厌烦。无论你的困扰或期望的结果是什么，都有相应的补充剂或智能应用程序。

但这些工具是为特定的身体设计的——也就是说，一个不包含另一个身体的身体。

在某些方面，我之前的生物黑客习惯为怀孕做了准备。我是一名超级马拉松跑者。所以当我的脚肿胀时，我有各种运动鞋可选——配有可扩展鞋带和宽脚趾箱的款式。我随时备有电解质储备。我听说怀孕甚至还有一点小小的运动优势：怀孕期间你的血液供应会增加。事实上，血容量增加高达50%。这基本上就是血液兴奋剂，当非孕妇这样做的时候。我曾热切地希望——也许过于热切——这能改善我产后首场比赛的成绩。（但实际上并没有。）

我期待服用产前维生素。我厨房台面的空间已经很紧张了，多亏了肌酸、肽、L-茶氨酸、铁补充剂等一堆瓶子。我有一个定制的ChatGPT项目，专门用于交叉检查补充剂和药物，确保没有禁忌反应。我还请ChatGPT帮助我根据怀孕期间的渴望来匹配营养需求。（咸菜是电解质的极佳来源。）

我在怀孕三个月时做了身体成分测试，六个月时又做了一次。我惊讶地看到我增肌了，这让我印象深刻！然后我意识到这可能不完全是“我自己的”肌肉。（如果我在另一个人身上产生了肌肉，我还能认为它属于我吗？）我当然感觉比以往任何时候都更“壮”。

怀孕期间的生物黑客改造并非全是乐趣和药片。从孕晚期开始，我开始遭受一种听起来比实际更有趣的病症：闪电胯。这是一种真正的医学术语，指的是婴儿头部压迫腹股沟神经时产生的射击样疼痛。（这也会是一个很好的变装皇后名字。）

有时产检感觉更像兄弟恶作剧而不是医疗检查。例如，常规超声波检查要求你带着充盈的膀胱来。最像兄弟恶作剧的是血糖测试。这是一项检测妊娠糖尿病的测试，医疗专业人员要求你在五分钟内喝下一瓶50或100克的糖。包装上写着“仅供处方使用”和“冷藏后口感更佳”。提供的口味是“橙子”。测试后你不允许走动。然后他们再次测试你的血液，看看血糖如何飙升。

当我显示出血糖测试的令人担忧的结果后，我被分配了一个连续血糖监测器。我很兴奋。许多生物黑客定期使用这些设备来了解他们的能量和情绪水平如何随着一天中血糖的反应而变化。我了解到，从医学上讲，我不是一个早起的人。我也了解到我没有妊娠糖尿病。

我可能过度改造了我的孕期。我的宝宝被诊断为LGA（大于胎龄儿）。我需要提前引产。是肌酸的原因还是仅仅是遗传？我们可能永远不会知道。

虽然我选择不在我的Oura Ring上记录怀孕，出于一种陌生的隐私欲望，但我确实戴着它进了产房。在我儿子出生后不久，我查看了我的手机。我的戒指应用程序，通常有一个阳光明媚的云朵背景，变成了一片不祥的红色风暴。在分娩过程中，一个红色警报弹出：“症状雷达：主要体征。你的生物特征显示有某些东西正在严重消耗你的身体。”在另一个角落：你的月经已经迟到277天了。它还检测到了一个“小睡”，可能是我硬膜外麻醉生效的时刻。

我曾被妈妈们警告过，在你刚生完孩子、还在服药和疲惫的时候，护士们会把大量的信息一股脑地扔给你。我决心不忘记任何事情，所以我用了一个Plaud AI笔记别针来转录整个过程。这份记录非常宝贵。但我的转录工具通常是为会议和工作会议设计的。所以这个自动生成的关于我孩子出生的摘要包括了“逆境中的精神韧性”作为一个“会议亮点”。

我从医院回家后，决定称一下体重。我感觉轻了，几乎感觉像一个新的人。事实上，我的智能体重秤拒绝相信我是同一个人。首先，它问我是否还好。然后它让我登出了。

怀孕在科学上被严重忽视，因为出于伦理原因缺乏随机对照试验。而生物黑客通常是一项孤独的事业。结果是，数十亿孕妇承受着预防原则的代价：避免一切，一无所知，并称之为安全。但我能够通过参加一项由美国国立卫生研究院赞助的研究来为科学理解做出贡献，该研究测量了我的行为如何影响我儿子的大脑发育。这是美国最大的早期大脑发育长期研究。它需要无尽的调查、功能磁共振成像，以及收集我的指甲屑，但为一项历史性的科学事业做出贡献感觉很好。他们还给我免费尿布。

我现在产后六个月了。我有一套新的工具可以玩。SNOO婴儿床会在检测到运动或声音时自动摇晃我的宝宝入睡。Huckleberry应用程序用人工智能追踪宝宝的睡眠，预测他的小睡时间。我现在同时在黑两个不同的生物系统。

最近我收到了Pulse的通知，这是ChatGPT的定制新闻源。它认为我应该读一篇文章：“当怀孕优化走得太远。”这篇文章讲述了我对该工具的过度使用可能表明我如此努力地想要控制做母亲的体验，以至于我实际上并没有在体验它。技术在这里告诉我不要使用太多的技术。

我已经和我的科技产品对话了一年。它终于在回应我了。它的信息是：放松点。

前几代人没有同样丰富的产品来减轻这一最具变革性和定义性的人类体验中的痛苦和风险。但神秘本身也有乐趣。现代科学有时会证实古老的认知方式。多年来，助产士一直告诉孕妇，胃灼热意味着宝宝会有更多的头发。科学最近记录了这种相关性，尽管我们仍然不知道原因。配方奶是母乳的一个极好的、往往是必要的补充和替代品，但只有母乳具有难以置信的适应性优势，能够实时响应宝宝的需求——晚上是褪黑素，白天是皮质醇，以及为你的环境定制的抗体。女性身体真的是一股自然力量。

到目前为止，我已经恢复了六块腹肌中的两块。但我一直在努力恢复整个腹肌。我对分娩的代价更加平和了，因为我知道这一切是为了什么。在某种程度上，生育是最终的生物黑客。女性不仅能够对自己的身体施加一定的控制，还能够控制是否有一个新的身体来到这个世界。而且这在某种程度上仍然是合法的——至少目前是这样。我现在向我聚会上遇到的每个人推荐生育，就像我以前推销肌酸一样。我的儿子让我大脑中的血清素比任何选择性5-羟色胺再摄取抑制剂都多。可以说，我生物黑客了我的灵魂。感觉非常理想。
articles.summary:
My six-pack began to disappear in the fourth month of my pregnancy, with the final pack officially vanishing in week 15.

My disappearing abs were just one of many demoralizing trade-offs my career as a biohacker took when I got pregnant. Since adolescence, I have been genetically blessed with visible abs. While I would like to credit my hard work, six-packs are mostly just the product of weight distribution and low body fat. You cannot control where your body adds fat, even subcutaneous fat. But optimizing my body's performance has been a delightful hobby since the lockdown, when I locked in on biohacking.

Side note: I don't use the phrase "we're pregnant" since Nick Gillespie, Reason editor at large and also my husband, endured very little of the pregnancy experience.

Pregnancy meant my commitment to biohacking was about to get more intense—even as the tools available were about to get less useful. I had become someone else's sensory deprivation tank. The biohacker had become the biohack-ee. Biohacking is the simple application of science and tech to change your body however you desire. A whole market has sprung up so that a whole new cadre of self-experimenters can be absolutely insufferable at parties. Whatever your ailment or desired outcome, there's a supplement or smart app for it. 

But these tools are meant for a certain body. Which is to say, a body that does not currently contain another body. 

In some ways, my previous biohacking habits had prepared me for pregnancy. I am an ultramarathon runner. So when my feet swelled, I had my choice of running shoes with expandable laces and a wide toe box. I had a reserve of electrolytes at the ready. I had heard there was even a small athletic advantage to being pregnant: Your blood supply increases during pregnancy. In fact, blood volume increases by up to 50 percent. This is basically blood doping, when done by nongestators. I hoped, perhaps a little too fervently, that this might improve my time in my first post-pregnancy race. (It did not.)

I was excited to take prenatal vitamins. Already, real estate on my kitchen countertop is scarce, thanks to bottles of creatine, peptides, L-theanine, iron supplements, etc. I had a custom ChatGPT project dedicated to cross-referencing supplements and drugs to make sure there were no contraindications. I also asked ChatGPT to help match my nutritional needs to pregnancy cravings. (Pickles are an excellent source of electrolytes.)

I took a body composition test at three months pregnant and took another at six months. I was surprised and impressed to see I had gained muscle! Then I realized it probably was not entirely "my" muscle. (If I made muscle attached to another human being, could I still consider it mine?) I certainly felt more "swoll" than ever before.

Biohacking my way through pregnancy wasn't all fun and pills, though. Starting in my third trimester, I began to suffer from a condition that sounds like more fun than it is: lightning crotch. This is an actual medical term for the shooting pain that occurs when the baby's head presses on a nerve in your groin. (It would also be a great drag name.)

Sometimes the doctor's visits for pregnancy felt more like a frat prank than a medical checkup. Routine ultrasounds require you to show up with a full bladder, for example. The most frat-ish was the blood glucose test. This is a test for gestational diabetes where a medical professional requires you to chug a bottle of 50 or 100 grams of sugar in five minutes. The packaging declares that the glucose drink is "for prescription use only" and "best served chilled." The flavor offered was "orange." You are not allowed to walk after the test. Then they test your blood again to see how your blood sugar spikes.

When I showed troubling results from the sugar chug test, I was assigned a continuous blood glucose monitor. I was jazzed. Many biohackers use these regularly to learn about how their energy and mood levels change throughout the day in response to blood sugar. I learned that I am, medically, not a morning person. Also that I do not have gestational diabetes. 

I may have overhacked my pregnancy. My baby was diagnosed with LGA (large for gestational age). I was going to be induced early. Was it the creatine or just genetics? We may never know.  

Though I chose not to log my pregnancy on my Oura Ring, out of a foreign desire for privacy, I did wear it into the delivery room. A little while after delivering my son, I checked my phone. My ring app, usually with a sunny cloud background, had become a foreboding red storm. During childbirth, a red alert popped up: "Symptom Radar: Major Signs. Your Biometrics show major signs of something straining your body." And in a separate corner: You are 277 days late for your period. It also detected a "nap," likely the moment when my epidural kicked in.

I had been warned by mothers that right after you give birth and are drugged up and tired, the nurses throw a million pieces of information at you. I was determined not to forget anything, so I used a Plaud AI note pin to transcribe the entire process. The record was invaluable. But my transcription tool is usually meant for conferences and work meetings. So the auto-generated summary of the birth of my child included "Mental Fortitude in Adversity" as a "Meeting Highlight."

After I got home from the hospital, I decided to weigh myself. I felt lighter, almost felt like a new person. In fact, my smart scale refused to believe I was the same person. First, it asked me if I was OK. Then it logged me out.

Pregnancy is criminally understudied due to the lack of randomized controlled trials for ethical reasons. And biohacking is usually a solitary venture. The result is that billions of pregnant women pay the price of the precautionary principle: avoid everything, know nothing, and call that safety. But I have been able to contribute to scientific understanding by enrolling in a study sponsored by the National Institutes of Health, which measured how my behavior affects my son's brain development. It's the largest long-term study of early brain development in the U.S. It entails endless surveys, functional magnetic resonance imaging, and collecting my fingernail clippings, but it feels good to contribute to a historical scientific venture. Also they give me free diapers. 

I am now six months postpartum. I have a new host of tools to play with. The SNOO bassinet automatically rocks my baby to sleep whenever it detects motion or sound. The Huckleberry app tracks my baby's sleep to predict his nap times with AI. I'm now hacking two different bios. 

I recently got a notification from Pulse, ChatGPT's custom newsfeed. It was an essay the AI thought I should read: "When pregnancy optimization goes too far." It was about how my excessive use of the tool might indicate that I was trying so hard to control the experience of motherhood that I was not actually experiencing it. Here was technology, telling me not to use so much technology.

I had been talking at my tech for a year. It was finally talking back. And its message was: chill out.

Previous generations did not have the same wealth of products to alleviate the suffering and risks of this most transformative and defining human experience. But there is also delight in mystery. Modern science sometimes confirms old ways of knowing. For years, midwives have told pregnant women that heartburn means the baby will have more hair. Science has recently documented this correlation, though we still don't know the cause. Formula is a fantastic and often necessary supplement and substitute for breastmilk, but only breastmilk has incredible adaptive advantages of responding in real time to your baby's needs—melatonin at night and cortisol in the day, and custom antibodies for your environment. The female body is truly a force of nature.

So far, I have regained two parts of my six-pack. But I have not been throwing myself at regaining the whole set. I am much more at peace with the costs of giving birth because I know what it was for. In some ways, reproduction is the ultimate biohack. Women can not only exert some control over their own bodies, but they can also control whether a new body comes into existence. And somehow this is still legal—so far. I now recommend procreation to everyone I meet at parties the same way I used to push creatine. My son has put more serotonin in my brain than any Selective Serotonin Reuptake Inhibitor ever has. One could say I biohacked my soul. And it feels optimal.

The post A Biohacker Gives Birth appeared first on Reason.com.

articles.title: L.A. Delays Its $30 Hotel 'Olympic Wage' Until After the Olympics
articles.content:
去年5月，洛杉矶市议会投票决定将市内酒店工人的最低工资提高至每小时30美元，于2028年生效。这标志着该市十多年来针对酒店行业提高最低工资的顶峰，但这些政策已经损害了酒店行业并减少了就业。

然而，上个月在最后一刻，市议会投票决定将30美元工资的实施时间推迟两年至2030年，为酒店行业争取到了暂时的喘息机会。虽然推迟总比没有好，但天使之城——以及全国各地的进步派政治家——应该借鉴洛杉矶酒店工资的经验教训，彻底反思他们错误的工资政策。

洛杉矶酒店最低工资的争议可以追溯到2015年，当时该市对住宿行业实施了每小时15.37美元的最低工资。该工资与通胀挂钩，意味着它随着时间稳步增长，到2025年已超过每小时21美元。作为对比，洛杉矶的常规最低工资在2016年为10.50美元，目前为17.87美元，定于7月1日上涨至18.42美元。

洛杉矶提高酒店工资的影响显而易见。自2015年以来，酒店行业的年同比就业增长率持续下降，从2014年的6.2%降至2024年的0.2%。到2025年12月，该行业就业下降1.7%，创下了十年来最大的年同比降幅。（该行业在2021年至2023年期间确实经历了快速就业增长，但这主要是因为2020年新冠疫情第一年旅游业遭受灾难性损失后的反弹。）

尽管面临这样的经济现实，洛杉矶市议会仍决定变本加厉。去年5月，议会颁布了更为激进的酒店最低工资法案。由于该工资计划与洛杉矶举办2028年奥运会的时间相吻合，被冠以"奥运工资"之名，议会投票决定到2028年将酒店工资提高至每小时30美元。

该行业的业主迅速对如此大幅工资上涨的潜在影响发出警告，尤其考虑到这一定价调整恰逢该市预期大量游客涌入之际。牛津经济公司2023年的一项分析预测，30美元的酒店工资将导致洛杉矶经济中近15000个工作岗位的流失。

美国酒店与住宿协会2026年1月的一项调查发现，88%的洛杉矶酒店在前一年进行了裁员或削减员工工时——这与市议会于2025年5月正式批准新工资上涨的时间相吻合。这表明酒店业主已经在工资上涨计划实施前采取措施削减劳动力成本。

一位知名酒店业主表示，他的公司已开始寻求退出洛杉矶酒店市场，他指出由于该市激进的最低工资立场，很难找到愿意收购酒店物业的买家。另一位业主则报告称正在增加人工智能的使用——通过机器人清洁和基于人工智能的客户服务——来抵消即将增加的劳动力成本。

鉴于这些影响，酒店和旅游业决定以牙还牙。从亚裔美国酒店业主协会到达美航空，各行业协会和旅游企业联合起来，成功将一项公投措施纳入2026年11月选举，旨在废除洛杉矶的总收入税。这将每年给洛杉矶造成超过8亿美元的损失，从而引发该市的财政危机。

"将需要裁员数千人，"洛杉矶市行政官马修·萨博在5月市议会的一次会议上表示。"该市将被迫实施比大萧条或新冠疫情期间更为严厉的紧缩措施。"萨博进一步预测将裁减约2000名警察，这将使洛杉矶为奥运会的准备工作"陷入严重危险"。

然而，公投措施的发起方表示，如果市议会同意推迟酒店工资上涨，他们愿意撤回该措施。面对这一潜在的灾难性威胁，市议会选择了让步。上个月，它投票决定将奥运工资推迟至2030年，让酒店行业多两年喘息之机。（该工资仍将作为分阶段增长方案逐步实施。）

但尽管这个问题在洛杉矶暂时得到平息，显而易见的是，高额酒店特定最低工资的概念正在蔓延。加州其他许多城市都效仿了洛杉矶的做法，圣莫尼卡、长滩和圣地亚哥都颁布了自己的酒店工资法案。阿纳海姆也考虑过类似提案，但最终被否决。

这不仅仅是加州的问题。纽约市目前正在考虑全市30美元的最低工资，而支持洛杉矶酒店奥运工资的知名地方工会UNITE HERE Local 11，则推动将30美元工资扩展至该市所有工人。

洛杉矶——以及更广泛的进步派——与其继续变本加厉地推行明显有害的工资上涨，不如借鉴奥运工资的经验，重新审视他们对30美元工资的全面推动。

《洛杉矶将30美元酒店"奥运工资"推迟至奥运会后》首发于Reason.com。
articles.summary:
In May of last year, the Los Angeles City Council voted to raise the minimum wage for hotel workers in the city to $30 an hour by 2028. This represented the culmination of over a decade of hotel-specific minimum wage rises in the city, which have hampered the hotel industry and reduced employment.

But last month, at the last minute, the city council voted to delay the enactment of the $30 wage by two years to 2030, buying a temporary reprieve for the hotel sector. While a delay is better than nothing, the City of Angels—and progressive politicians across the country—should use the experience of L.A.'s hotel wage to revisit their misguided wage policies entirely.

The drama over L.A.'s hotel minimum wage dates back to 2015, when the city enacted a $15.37 minimum wage for the lodging sector. The wage was indexed to inflation, meaning that it steadily increased over time, reaching just over $21 an hour by 2025. For comparison, L.A.'s regular minimum wage was $10.50 in 2016 and stands at $17.87 today, with an increase to $18.42 scheduled to take place on July 1.

The impact of L.A.'s heightened hotel wage has been palpable. Year-over-year employment growth in the hotel industry has declined since 2015, going from 6.2 percent growth in 2014 to 0.2 percent growth in 2024. It fell to -1.7 percent in December 2025, which made for the largest year-over-year decline in a decade. (The industry did experience rapid employment growth from 2021 through 2023, though this was driven by a rebound from the cataclysmic losses across the tourism industry in 2020, the first year of the COVID-19 pandemic.)

Despite this economic reality, L.A.'s city council decided to double down. Last May, the council enacted an even more supersized hotel minimum wage. Dubbed the "Olympic wage"—since it was set to correlate with L.A. hosting the 2028 Olympics—the council voted to increase hotel wages to $30 an hour by 2028.

Business owners in the industry quickly sounded the alarm about the potential effects of such a drastic wage hike, timed to hit hardest right when the city was expecting a massive influx of tourists. A 2023 analysis by Oxford Economics predicted that a $30 hotel wage would lead to job losses of almost 15,000 in Los Angeles' economy.

A January 2026 survey from the American Hotel and Lodging Association found that 88 percent of Los Angeles hotels had undergone layoffs or cut hours for staff during the prior year—which coincides with the city council officially approving the new wage hike in May 2025. This suggests that hotel owners were already taking steps to reduce labor costs ahead of the planned wage increases.

One prominent hotel owner, whose company began looking to exit the L.A. hotel scene, noted that it was difficult to find any willing buyers of hotel property due to the city's aggressive minimum wage stance. Another owner reported increasing the use of AI—via robotic cleaning and AI-based customer relations—to offset looming labor costs.

Given these impacts, the hotel and tourism industry decided to fight fire with fire. A broad array of trade associations and travel businesses—from the Asian American Hotel Owners Association to Delta Airlines—managed to qualify a ballot measure for the November 2026 election to repeal L.A.'s gross receipts tax. That would cost L.A. over $800 million annually, thereby triggering a fiscal crisis for the city.

"Thousands of layoffs would be required, said Matthew Szabo, L.A.'s city administrative officer, at a May city council meeting. "The city would be forced to implement austerity measures far worse than seen during the Great Recession or the COVID-19 pandemic." Szabo went on to predict the layoff of around 2,000 police officers, which would put L.A.'s preparation for the Olympic games "in severe jeopardy."

The backers of the ballot measure offered to withdraw it, however, if the city council agreed to delay the hotel wage hike. In the face of this potentially catastrophic threat, the city council stood down. Last month, it voted to delay the Olympic wage to 2030, sparing the hotel industry for a couple more years. (It will still operate as a phased-in increase with graduated annual hikes.)

But while the issue has been temporarily quelled in L.A., it's clear that the concept of high hotel-specific minimum wages is only spreading. A host of other California cities have followed L.A.'s lead, with Santa Monica, Long Beach, and San Diego enacting their own hotel wages. A similar proposal was considered but ultimately rejected in Anaheim.

And it isn't just California. New York City is currently considering a citywide $30 minimum wage, while UNITE HERE Local 11, a prominent local labor union that supported L.A.'s Olympic wage for hotels, has pushed to extend the $30 wage to all workers in the city.

Rather than continuing to double down on clearly harmful wage hikes, L.A.—and progressives writ large—should use the experience of the Olympic wage to reconsider their push for $30 altogether.

The post L.A. Delays Its $30 Hotel 'Olympic Wage' Until After the Olympics appeared first on Reason.com.
